name: Horse Shoe Rocks
description: This dive site features dramatic granite boulders forming arches and swim-throughs, with visibility often exceeding 30 meters. Schools of batfish and trevally patrol the area, while eagle rays glide past the rocky outcrops. The site's shallow plateau hosts vibrant soft corals and anemones teeming with clownfish. Strong currents attract pelagic species, making it ideal for drift diving. Depths range from 12 to 25 meters, suitable for all experience levels. The clear, warm waters showcase Seychelles' rich biodiversity.

name: Fusilier Bank
description: A submerged pinnacle rising from deep waters, this site teems with schooling fusiliers, trevally, and occasional reef sharks. The steep walls are covered in soft corals and sponges, with strong currents attracting pelagic life. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters, making it ideal for drift diving. Depths range from 12 to 40 meters, suitable for experienced divers. The remote location ensures pristine conditions.

name: Amoujie Mamon
description: This dive site in the Seychelles offers stunning granite formations covered in soft corals and vibrant marine life. Expect strong currents that attract pelagics like sharks and rays. The underwater landscape features dramatic drop-offs and swim-throughs teeming with colorful reef fish. Visibility is often excellent, making it ideal for photographers. The site is best suited for experienced divers due to the challenging conditions.

name: L'ilot Rocks
description: L'Ilot Rocks offers vibrant coral gardens teeming with reef fish, moray eels, and occasional reef sharks. The site features dramatic granite boulders creating swim-throughs and overhangs, perfect for exploration. Clear waters with visibility often exceeding 30 meters make it ideal for photography. Gentle currents attract schools of trevally and barracuda. Depths range from 5 to 25 meters, suitable for all experience levels. The site is sheltered, providing calm conditions year-round. Nearby, colorful soft corals and sponges cover the rocks, creating a stunning underwater landscape.

name: Lapay Koray
description: A stunning granite boulder dive off Mahe Island, teeming with vibrant marine life. Schools of batfish and trevally swirl around the rocks, while moray eels peek from crevices. The sloping reef transitions to sand at 25m, perfect for spotting stingrays and octopus. Clear visibility and mild currents make it ideal for all skill levels. The dramatic rock formations create swim-throughs and overhangs, adding excitement to every dive.

name: Coral Garden
description: This vibrant reef off Mahe Island teems with colorful corals, schooling fish, and occasional reef sharks. Gentle currents make it ideal for beginners, while deeper sections reveal dramatic drop-offs. Expect excellent visibility and warm waters year-round. Turtles and eagle rays frequent the area, adding excitement to every dive. The shallow sections burst with life, perfect for snorkelers too.

name: De Tete
description: This dive site in the Seychelles offers stunning granite formations covered in soft corals. Expect strong currents that attract pelagic species like sharks and barracuda. The clear waters provide excellent visibility, often exceeding 30 meters. The dramatic underwater landscape features swim-throughs and overhangs teeming with colorful reef fish. Best suited for experienced divers due to the challenging conditions.

name: Light House
description: This dive site off Mahe Island features dramatic granite boulders and swim-throughs teeming with marine life. Expect strong currents attracting pelagics like eagle rays and reef sharks. The shallow coral gardens host vibrant fish, while deeper sections reveal impressive drop-offs. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters, making it ideal for photographers. Best dived at slack tide due to the strong surge.
